factors.summary: Demand for commercial rental services is weakening.

evidence.text: Commercial rental utilization — power fleet (3) 70.4 % 73.4 % (300) bps
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The weakening demand for commercial rental services is indicated by a significant 300 basis point decline in utilization.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: Full service lease revenue increased 8% in the first quarter of 2016 reflecting a 5% larger average fleet size and higher prices on replacement vehicles.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully raised prices, leading to an 8% increase in full service lease revenue.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Contract-related maintenance revenue increased 21% reflecting favorable impacts from growth in the full service lease fleet and higher volumes.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, evidenced by a 21% increase in contract-related maintenance revenue.

evidence.text: Operating revenue (revenue excluding fuel) increased 7% in the first quarter of 2016 as a result of organic growth, primarily in the full service lease product line.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with a 7% increase in operating revenue.

evidence.text: Average commercial rental power fleet size — in service (3), (4) 32,900 31,400 5 %
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, as shown by a 5% increase in the average commercial rental power fleet size.

factors.summary: The dedicated transportation solutions market is growing fastest.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 15% to $244.8 million, reflecting increased revenue from organic growth and subcontracted transportation.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The dedicated transportation solutions market is growing fastest, contributing to a 15% increase in total revenue.

factors.summary: Input costs are declining due to lower fuel prices.

evidence.text: Fuel services revenue declined 28% in the three months ended March 31, 2016 due to lower fuel prices passed through to customers.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are declining due to lower fuel prices, leading to a 28% decline in fuel services revenue.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding.

evidence.text: Segment EBT increased 59% in the first quarter of 2016 due to the benefits of higher operating revenue and to a lesser extent lower insurance costs.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding, with segment EBT increasing 59% in the first quarter of 2016.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Full service lease and commercial rental results benefited from approximately $9 million of lower depreciation in the first quarter of 2016 due to residual value changes implemented January 1, 2016, partially offset by increased insurance costs due to unfavorable self-insurance developments from prior years.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Measurable savings from cost reduction programs indicate progress, though partially offset by increased costs.

evidence.text: Contract maintenance revenue increased 9% reflecting a 13% larger average fleet size.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in operational efficiency is evident from increased contract maintenance revenue.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a headwind with weaker demand in certain segments.

evidence.text: We expect unfavorable commercial rental revenue comparisons through the end of the year based on a projected weaker demand environment.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Weaker demand is expected to negatively impact commercial rental revenue.

factors.summary: Management expects favorable comparisons through the end of the year.

evidence.text: We expect favorable full service lease revenue comparisons to continue through the end of the year based on strong sales activity.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management anticipates favorable revenue comparisons due to strong sales activity.

factors.summary: Management is confident with strong sales activity and expected growth.

evidence.text: We expect favorable full service lease revenue comparisons to continue through the end of the year based on strong sales activity.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong sales activity and growth expectations reflect high management confidence.

factors.summary: Risks include weaker demand and unfavorable foreign exchange impacts.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted both total and operating revenue growth in the first quarter of 2016 by 100 basis points.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange impacts pose a significant near-term risk.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange and fuel prices are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted full service lease revenue growth by 100 basis points. Fuel margins in the prior year benefited from the timing of fuel purchases in a period of rapidly declining fuel prices.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange and fuel prices are key sensitivities with current negative impacts.

factors.summary: New business and higher pricing are catalysts for growth.

evidence.text: Operating revenue (revenue excluding subcontracted transportation and fuel) increased 9% to $322 million in the first quarter of 2016. Operating revenue growth was due to new business, increased volumes and higher pricing.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: New business and higher pricing are driving strong revenue growth.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are planned to decrease.

evidence.text: We expect full-year 2016 capital expenditures to be approximately $2.0 billion. We expect to fund 2016 capital expenditures primarily with internally generated funds and additional debt financing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The planned decrease in capital expenditures indicates a cautious approach, which is a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is manageable with strong access to capital markets.

evidence.text: Our debt ratings and rating outlooks at March 31, 2016 were as follows: Short-term Rating Outlook Long-term Rating Outlook Moody’s Investors Service P2 Stable Baa1 Stable Standard & Poor’s Ratings Services A2 Stable BBB Positive Fitch Ratings F2 Stable A- Stable

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong access to capital markets and stable ratings suggest manageable debt leverage, a positive development.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates improving free cash flow, despite the negative value provided, suggesting a positive trend.
